description_long: This is a terracotta vase with a bulbous body and a distinctive double-looped handle that arches above the vessel. The surface is decorated with swirling and linear white patterns over a reddish-orange base, giving it a hand-painted, rustic appearance. The spout protrudes from the lower part of the body, while the handle is thick and robust, merging smoothly into the vessel's shoulder. The white decorative lines spiral around the body and are complemented by vertical stripes, creating a dynamic visual effect. The finish appears matte and slightly rough, typical of traditional earthenware. This vase would be a striking decorative piece in an urban park, public plaza, or as part of a street art installation.
description: A terracotta vase with a double-looped handle, reddish-orange body, and white spiral and stripe patterns, featuring a matte, rustic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout and the central spiral pattern, with the double-looped handle visible above.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thick handle and the vertical white stripes running down the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full spiral motif and the continuous white lines wrapping around the vase.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle and spout both visible, emphasizing the striped pattern.
